rag_rules: Preserve AADL structure and insert annex content only within the owning component or implementation scope.
Keep type-level contract content in component type scope; keep implementation-level bindings only in implementation scope.
Do not move, rename, or alter existing subcomponents, connections, features, or properties when adding annex content.
generic_rules: Maintain owner/scope consistency between the generated annex block and the element that owns it.
Only reference names that are valid in the selected scope; avoid relying on subcomponent internals from a broader scope.
Limit edits to the minimal structural change needed to attach the provided annex content while preserving architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Implementation scope is appropriate for implementation-level bindings; contract clauses belong in component type scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place type-scope contract statements under an implementation owner.
Do not introduce or modify architecture elements just to support annex insertion.
Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as in-scope AGREE identifiers unless explicitly exposed.
irrelevant_topics: State-expression syntax details such as temporal recurrence and nested conditional expressions are not needed for owner/scope fusion.
